heading:
Prove what you made.
Keep what's yours.
lead: Every image, PDF, or video you export can carry a **Content Credential** - a tamper-evident [C2PA](https://c2pa.org) seal created entirely on your device. It proves the file hasn't changed since it left Lolly, and once you enrol an identity, records who signed it. Rasters also carry the invisible **Lolly Imprint** in the pixels themselves, on by default, so a copy can still be recognised even after the credential is stripped.

checks.title: Tamper-evident by default
checks.desc: The seal covers every exported byte. Change one pixel and any C2PA validator flags it.

checks.title: Signed on your device
checks.desc: Your signing key is generated locally and never leaves the device - not even Lolly can read it.

checks.title: Verify anything, on-device
checks.desc: The Verify tab checks a file's credential, flags AI-generated content, reads the Lolly Imprint, and surfaces hidden data - offline, nothing uploaded.

cards.title: Nothing leaves your device
cards.desc: No cloud rendering, no telemetry, no analytics. What you make stays on your machine.

cards.title: Scrub and lock, on-device
cards.desc: Strip Hidden Data removes EXIF and metadata locally. Lock a PDF, a download, or a share link with a password that never leaves your device.

cards.title: Self-host or air-gap
cards.desc: No server, no database, no backend. Run entirely behind your firewall, fully offline.
